title: CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Human Review Queue Depth for Adverse Automated Eligibility Decisions

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → '§1798.185'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="privacy_automated_decision_review" state!="Closed" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age_h=round((now()-opened)/3600,1)
| where age_h>72
| stats avg(age_h) as avg_age_h, max(age_h) as max_age_h, count by assignment_group
| sort - count
value: When automated decisions adversely affect consumers, human review pathways must function. Monitoring queue age for appeals ensures operational readiness beyond policy language—supporting transparency and remedy expectations.
implementation: (1) Map `cat_item` to your catalog entry; (2) tune SLA hours per CPRA operational policy; (3) executive dashboard for legal; (4) integrate with chat alerts; (5) monthly remediation report.
visualization: Table (queues), Single value (oldest age_h), Column chart by group.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
